summary: Stellar Dark Star/Eyes jam, crystal‑clear matrix sound, tight set flow
blurb: The show shines with a soaring Dark Star > Eyes Of The World > Stella Blue suite and a beautiful Bird Song. Jerry delivers soaring solos while Phil’s deep bass anchors the jams; Keith’s piano adds twinkling texture. The matrix blend recording is excellent, offering balanced board‑audience mix and crisp drums.
review: June 30, 1973 at the Universal Amphitheatre captures the Dead in a classic summer groove. The first set rolls through bright country‑rock numbers and builds to an inspired Playing In The Band, but it’s the second set that truly dazzles – a Dark Star that lifts into an expansive Eyes Of The World jam before resolving into a heartfelt Stella Blue, all underpinned by Phil’s warm, thunderous bass. Bird Song shines as a lyrical highlight, while Sugar Magnolia and the one‑drummer Black Peter encore keep the energy high. Across the board, Jerry’s guitar work is described as “soaring” and “perfect‑tone,” Keith’s piano weaves delicate textures, and Bill Kreutzmann delivers crisp, tight rhythms that drive the improvisations forward. The matrix blend (gd1973-06-30.sbd.sbvol9.chappell.29753.flac16) offers crystal‑clear balance of board and audience, deep bass and immaculate drum presence, making this the definitive listening source for a show that many fans rank among the year’s best.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Eight of the eleven recordings are uniformly positive, repeatedly praising Dark Star/Eyes jam, Bird Song, and Playing In The Band. Multiple sources use words like “soaring,” “crystal‑clear,” and “stellar.” Only two recordings offer mixed comments about tape quality, not performance, and one negative note concerns a poor audience transfer. Consistent high praise across the majority of analyses justifies a strong positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Matrix blend delivers crystal‑clear balance of board and audience, deep bass and crisp drums – the most listenable version.

key_highlights: Dark Star > Eyes Of The World > Stella Blue suite is the show’s centerpiece.
Bird Song provides a lyrical, beautifully phrased moment in the first set.
Playing In The Band showcases tight band interplay and soaring solos.
Matrix blend recording offers exceptional sound quality with balanced mix.

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers soaring solos and soulful guitar tone, especially on Dark Star and the Eyes jam; vocal delivery praised despite occasional mix‑level issues.
band_performance.Phil: Warm, deep bass that anchors the improvisations, dropping "bombs" during jams and providing classic 1973 groove.
band_performance.Bob: Adds vocal fireworks, notably on Black Peter; overall solid rhythm guitar work throughout.
band_performance.Keith: Twinkling piano adds delicate texture, intertwining with Jerry’s lead; Moog/Rhodes tones noted as wonderful color.
band_performance.Billy: Crisp, tight drumming that drives the jams; highlighted on one‑drummer Black Peter and throughout the high‑energy set.
